title: tragopogon pratensis subsp. grandiflorus
intro: Tragopogon pratensis subsp. grandiflorus, also known as the Great Sow Thistle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as protecting against cell damage. The health benefits of Tragopogon pratensis subsp. grandiflorus include its potential to help lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
